taskDescription: Sprint: Parallel Execution — implement the plan at docs/execution/plans/sprint-parallel-execution.md. Two parallel tracks: Track 1 (training pipeline) and Track 2 (data/eval/notebooks). Current branch: main (sprint branch to be created). Key missing deliverables: scripts/hpc/run_sft.py (the SFT training launcher replacing the stub in submit_sft.sbatch), configs/smoke_sft_local.yaml, preflight.sh --local flag, and notebook 09 fill. Pre-work also needed: wave-d retrospective doc and branch creation.

features.description: PRE-WORK: Create sprint branch (git checkout -b sprint/parallel-execution && git push -u origin sprint/parallel-execution) and write docs/learn/project/wave-d-design-choices.md summarizing design choices from the Wave D implementation: lora_r=32 rationale, target_modules choice, apply_loss_mask API contract, keep-last-3-plus-best checkpoint policy, QualityFilter solver-confidence gate, smoke=50/cost-cap=$20 limits. Then discard CRLF drift in .claude/orchestrator/workers/feature-*.prompt via git checkout.

T1.1: Create scripts/hpc/run_sft.py (~200 LOC). CLI training launcher that: parses --config <yaml> with extends: chain resolution; loads base model + tokenizer (trust_remote_code=True, dtype=bfloat16, device_map=auto, optional load_in_4bit for QLoRA); wraps with peft.LoraConfig using lora_r/lora_alpha/lora_dropout/target_modules from config; loads pre-tokenized .pt shards from --data-dir using a passthrough DataCollator that respects existing labels tensor (NO re-masking); instantiates trl.SFTTrainer with TrainingArguments from config; supports --resume-from-checkpoint; calls checkpoint_policy.py after training; exits 0 on success, 1 on failure. Must NOT call apply_loss_mask (already applied during tokenization). Add assert that sampled batch has (labels == -100).any().

T1.2: Edit scripts/hpc/submit_sft.sbatch lines 122-162. Replace the entire python heredoc stub block with: RESUME_ARG detection via resume_from_latest.py, then call python scripts/hpc/run_sft.py --config $CONFIG_PATH --data-dir $TOKENIZED_DIR --output-dir $RUN_DIR $RESUME_ARG 2>&1 | tee -a $RUN_DIR/train.log. Keep surrounding env-var setup, log header, exit-code handling. Remove 'stub' language from top comment.

T1.3: Create configs/smoke_sft_local.yaml extending configs/smoke_sft.yaml with overrides: base_model: Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct, lora_r: 8, lora_alpha: 16, target_modules: [q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj], dataset_max_rows: 100, max_steps: 20, save_steps: 10, header comment 'LOCAL SMOKE ONLY — NOT FOR SUBMISSION'. Also edit scripts/hpc/preflight.sh to add --local flag that skips frozen BASE_MODEL_ID check and model-weights-path check while still running CUDA/disk/package/dataset SHA256 checks.

T2.4: Fill notebooks/09_sft_runbook_and_masking.ipynb beyond its current stub (~200 lines). Add cells: (1) masking walkthrough loading a real SFTExample, showing tokenizer output, calling apply_loss_mask, rendering which positions are kept vs masked; (2) pipeline diagram markdown: preflight → tokenize_dataset → run_sft → checkpoint_policy → regression_gate → package_adapter; (3) reference CLI invocation cells (do NOT execute training, just show commands for run_sft.py with lora_baseline.yaml and the sbatch submission); (4) resume semantics showing how resume_from_latest.py feeds --resume-from-checkpoint; (5) promotion criteria: golden gate must pass before package_adapter.py runs. Update docs/execution/NOTEBOOKS.md: notebook 09 status scaffolded → active.

T2.5: Run synthetic data smoke generation. Execute: python -m src.data.synthetic --smoke --dry-run (cost estimate), then python -m src.data.synthetic --smoke (50-row real generation). This produces data/processed/synthetic_train.jsonl + .sha256. If data/analysis/retry_candidates.jsonl is missing (expected), the smoke path uses an empty-list fallback. Document the smoke output in docs/execution/NOTEBOOKS.md notebook 08 entry if status needs updating.
